name: Gearbox service panel with one inaccessible screw
intent: Represent a synthetic service panel where one repeated screw remains accessible while its sibling is obstructed by an installed cable tray.

parts.name: Left service-panel screw
parts.purpose: Retain the left side of the service panel

fastener.basis: Declared by the authored BOM for this synthetic failure fixture.

parts.name: Right service-panel screw
parts.purpose: Retain the right side of the service panel

fastener.basis: Declared by the authored BOM for this synthetic failure fixture.

parts.name: Installed cable tray
parts.purpose: Carry the gearbox feedback and motor cables above the left screw

tool_access_cones.name: Left service-panel screw driver approach
tool_access_cones.rationale: The nominal upward approach is occupied by the installed cable tray.

tool_access_cones.name: Right service-panel screw driver approach
tool_access_cones.rationale: The right screw has an unobstructed upward service approach in the declared arrangement.

assumptions: This is an authored synthetic failure fixture; it does not represent downloaded or external source CAD.
The two repeated M4 screws are expanded into separate physical instances with separate access checks.
Every envelope is an intentionally conservative axis-aligned proxy in millimetres.
A clear result for the right screw remains bounded and does not prove physical access or fit.
